issue_title: datafileで対象がフォルダの場合を吸収する
Missing dependency in requirements.txt
issue_body:
`pythonz-bd` is specified as a dependency in [`setup.py`](https://github.com/berdario/pew/blob/master/setup.py#L26) but is missing in [`requirements.txt`](https://github.com/berdario/pew/blob/master/requirements.txt).
Also `pytest` should probably be in a separate `dev-requirements.txt`.

A solution to keep those requirements in sync is to put the following in your `setup.py` :
```
with open(os.path.join(os.path.dirname(os.path.realpath(__file__)), 'requirements.txt')) as file_requirements:
    requirements = file_requirements.readlines()
```
Just make sure the `requirements.txt` is included in the Pypi package (using `MANIFEST.in`).

issue_title: [Feature request] queueing + shuffle
issue_body:
Disc: long post, just skip to suggestions if you aren't up for some squawking.

Hi,

First of all, this is the best mopidy spotify interface around. I have two suggestions which "feels" like depending on one another.

**Whining no.1**: One of the biggest features I miss from the official spotify client is to have the rest of the playlist get queued when I double click on a track, so that it'd keep playing without any further interaction. Right now, the d-click behaviour is to place that track at the top of the play queue in spotmop. the problem: it won't continue playing unless I add the rest of the playlist by hand. The only way to have that is to queue the whole list by clicking "Play" in the playlist details view and skip to the desired track, which is impractical in true sense. Moreover, adding track to the top does that it wouldn't get played as you might already be playing a track somewhere deep in the queue and you need to go all the way back.

**Suggestion no.1**: double click should: clear the queue, add rest of the playlist to the queue, restart playing from the top of the queue.

**Whining no.2**: The player lacks some of the fundamental features a music player should have: play modes and time indicators. Relating to the first issue, it's nearly impossible to have a pleasant experience without constant interaction, since you'd need to skip frequently or hand curate the queue. And it'd actually be a strong "selling point" to have an alternative to infamously lame spotify official shuffling algo. The track time and remaining time indicators are a must too, imho. That'd also allude to a revamp of left frame, possibly with more lines(vertical space) allocated to play controls at the bottom. 

**Suggestion no.2**:  play modes like repeating and shuffling should be implemented for a better playing experience.

issue_title: ldap3 is incompatible with Python 3.7
issue_body:
`async` and `await` have become keywords in Python 3.7, and this breaks ldap3 because you can no longer have an `async.py` module.  This is a new `SyntaxError`:

```
    from .core.connection import Connection
E     File "venv/lib/python3.7/site-packages/ldap3/core/connection.py", line 53
E       from ..strategy.async import AsyncStrategy
E                           ^
E   SyntaxError: invalid syntax
```

I think you're going to have no choice but to rename that module unfortunately.  It's also unfortunate that `DeprecationWarnings` are disabled by default because if they weren't you would have seen that warning in Python 3.6:

```
% python3.6 -W error::DeprecationWarning
Python 3.6.3 (default, Oct  4 2017, 06:09:15) 
[GCC 4.2.1 Compatible Apple LLVM 9.0.0 (clang-900.0.37)] on darwin
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> import async
DeprecationWarning: 'async' and 'await' will become reserved keywords in Python 3.7
>>> 
```

issue_title: Use shell to pip to extrac checksums
issue_body:
We currently rely on [`_hash_of_file`](https://github.com/peterbe/hashin/blob/77e2d93f40f7b48ea147f5b032e517cada70b96c/hashin.py#L366) to extract the checksum of a package using pip. Yuck! We should use a shell and call like this:
```python
subprocess.call(['python', '-m', 'pip', 'hash', ...])
```

Thanks @di for the tip!

issue_title: No definitions found for certain scenario
issue_body:
Please see a detailed description and discussion in tkf/emacs-jedi#281, which turns out to be probably a Jedi issue. In brief:

1. Clone [this repository](https://github.com/ethz-asl/kalibr): `git clone https://github.com/ethz-asl/kalibr`
2. Go to [line 49](https://github.com/ethz-asl/kalibr/blob/master/aslam_offline_calibration/kalibr/python/kalibr_imu_camera_calibration/IccCalibrator.py#L49) and try to find definitions of `addDesignVariables`. No definitions are found, yet the `addDesignVariables` function is defined in [IccSensors.py](https://github.com/ethz-asl/kalibr/blob/master/aslam_offline_calibration/kalibr/python/kalibr_imu_camera_calibration/IccSensors.py#L316), which is a python file in the same directory.

Here is a Python console session showing the fault (courtesy [syohex](https://github.com/syohex) - which shows that I'm not the only one affected):
```
% ipython
Python 2.7.10 (default, Oct 23 2015, 19:19:21)
Type "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.

IPython 5.1.0 -- An enhanced Interactive Python.
?         -> Introduction and overview of IPython's features.
%quickref -> Quick reference.
help      -> Python's own help system.
object?   -> Details about 'object', use 'object??' for extra details.

In [1]: import jedi

In [2]: source = open('IccCalibrator.py', 'r').read()

In [3]: script = jedi.Script(source, 49, len('        self.CameraChain.'), 'IccCalibrator.py')

In [4]: defs = script.goto_definitions()

In [5]: defs
Out[5]: []
```

I am running Ubuntu 14.04 and:
```
$ pip show jedi
---
Name: jedi
Version: 0.10.0
Location: /usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages
Requires: 
$ pip show python
---
Name: Python
Version: 2.7
Location: /usr/lib/python2.7/lib-dynload
Requires: 
```

Since [line 177](https://github.com/ethz-asl/kalibr/blob/master/aslam_offline_calibration/kalibr/python/kalibr_calibrate_imu_camera#L177) calls the [registerCamChain](https://github.com/ethz-asl/kalibr/blob/master/aslam_offline_calibration/kalibr/python/kalibr_imu_camera_calibration/IccCalibrator.py#L58) function with `camChain` (an `IccCameraChain` object) argument, Jedi should make the guess that `self.CameraChain` is an `IccCameraChain` object and thus should be able to find the definition of `addDesignVariables` on [line 49](https://github.com/ethz-asl/kalibr/blob/master/aslam_offline_calibration/kalibr/python/kalibr_imu_camera_calibration/IccCalibrator.py#L49), which is a method of the `IccCameraChain` class.

issue_title: ip route replace support
issue_body:
Guess this is more of a feature request, but I just started taking a look at this library recently, thanks for writing it.

One thing some scripts I work on do is to call 'ip route replace...' instead of ever calling 'ip route add...' since replace will do an add if there isn't an existing entry, else change it.  It helps with having to check first, and eliminates the possibility of duplicate routes.

The flags used in this case are (NLM_F_CREATE|NLM_F_REPLACE).

My only other comment is that although this is a library, it does closely mimic what /sbin/ip does, so was a little confused why 'set' was chosen instead of 'change', but that's a nit and possibly just something the user has to manage.

issue_title: iproute.flush_route deletes more than asked
issue_body:
Hi Peter, long time to see !  :)

I've just hit the following bug in pyroute2:
```
>>> import pyroute2
>>> ipr = pyroute2.IPRoute()  # pylint: disable=no-member
>>> 
>>> for r in ipr.get_routes(table=254):
...    print r
... 
{'family': 2, 'dst_len': 24, 'proto': 2, 'tos': 0, 'event': 'RTM_NEWROUTE', 'header': {'pid': 6870, 'length': 60, 'flags': 2, 'error': None, 'type': 24, 'sequence_number': 261}, 'flags': 0, 'attrs': [('RTA_TABLE', 254), ('RTA_DST', '240.97.132.0'), ('RTA_PREFSRC', '240.97.132.200'), ('RTA_OIF', 374)], 'table': 254, 'src_len': 0, 'type': 1, 'scope': 253}
>>> ipr.flush_routes(family=pyroute2.common.AF_MPLS)
>>> for r in ipr.get_routes(table=254):
...        print r
... 
[nothing]
```

So, _ipr.flush_routes(family=pyroute2.common.AF_MPLS)_ deleted all the routes, the _family_ filter was not taken into account.  

I've observed the same issue with _proto_ as a filter to _flush_routes_.

I've had a look at the code, and nothing obvious stands out, flush_routes seems to rely on get_routes that *does* properly take into account kwargs to filter.

issue_title: Still doesn't work with Python 3.6
issue_body:
https://github.com/tiran/defusedxml/commit/1d342237b560e29e8401d0a22a776b52b09e0ae2 is not sufficient to make defusedxml work properly with Python 3.6. It stops defusedxml outright choking in `_get_py3_cls`, but it doesn't *work* properly. It results in `common._generate_etree_functions()` failing, as can be seen in the 10 failed tests from the Travis run for that commit.

I naively attempted just changing the `if PY26 or PY31:` condition in `_generate_etree_functions()` to `if PY26 or PY31 or sys.version_info >= (3, 6):` , but that's not good enough either because that code really is tailored to the Python 2.6 / 3.1 implementation; it doesn't work at all for the Python 3.6 implementation. It seems like we need an entirely new approach to defusing iterparse for 3.6, but I'm not smart enough to write that. At least, not at this time in the morning...

This is a bit of a problem for us (Fedora) as Python 3.6 landed in Rawhide today, and now defusedxml won't install and can't be rebuilt (unless we ignore the fact that it's broken and disable the tests...), and several things that depend on it can't install or be rebuilt either.

issue_title: Consumers get stuck with no new data, while there are unread messages
issue_body:
This is an interesting one.

In all of our topics every day a handful of partitions get "stuck".
Basically the reading of the partition stops at a given message and kafka-python reports that there are no more messages in the given partition (just like it would have consumed all messages), while there are unconsumed messages.
The only way to get the consumers moving again is to manually seek the offset forward by stepping over the "stuck" messages and then works again for a few million records and then get stuck again at some later offset.

I have multiple consumers consuming from the same topic and they all get stuck at the same messages of the same topics. Random number of partitions are affected day-to-day.

We are using Kafka broker version 0.9.0.1, kafka-python 1.2.1 (had the same issue with 1.1.1).

The consumer code is very simple (the below code is trying to read only partition #1, which is currently "stuck"):

```
kafka_consumer = KafkaConsumer(
    group_id=kafka_group_id,
    bootstrap_servers=kafka_servers,
    enable_auto_commit=False,
    consumer_timeout_ms=10000,
    fetch_max_wait_ms=10*1000,
    request_timeout_ms=10*1000
)
topics = [TopicPartition(topic, 1)]
kafka_consumer.assign(topics)

for message in kafka_consumer:
    print(message)

print("Completed")
```

The above code prints "Completed", but not the messages, while there is a 5M offset lag in partition 1, so there would be plenty of messages to read.
After seeking the consumer offset forward the code works again until it doesn't get "stuck" again.

issue_title: Github pages documentation error regarding submitting a form
issue_body:
The form submit example present at http://jeanphix.me/Ghost.py/#form contains the line:
``` python
page, resources = ghost.fire_on("form", "submit", expect_loading=True)
```
However, `fire_on` seems to have been replaced with `call` (in commit 2cb9ca467dd744be8f843249a6f70767e72ee11a).

Therefore, the correct line would be:
```python
page, resources = ghost.call("form", "submit", expect_loading=True)
```

issue_title: Issue with some characters
issue_body:
hi,

I isolated an issue when using for example:
```
u'& &'
```
In an object. Then the key-value pair is missing in the hash (when I look in redis). By missing, I mean that when the object is persisted, that key-value pair (where the value is `& &` does not get written). As a result, the (partially) written object cannot be read.

I tested manually and redis has no issues with it. If I input the value directly in redis, loading the object that was previously crashing now loads.

It seems to be an issue only on write. I am not sure where the issue comes from, though. There is no error or anything written. It seems like the key-value just gets "skipped".

This is the configuration of the column:
`rom.Text(required=True, index=True, keygen=rom.FULL_TEXT, prefix=True,)`

It does seem also that these characters `&` get dropped by the indexer. Is it possible that it gets skipped because each character of the string got skipped by the indexer?

The error only manifests on read: then the object is incomplete and cannot be loaded.

Please let me know if you have any thoughts and/or if you can help.

Thank you in advance.

issue_title: fix md5 etag mismatch on response
issue_body:
I think there are a couple of options here. 

1) Simplest is to drop the etag header from the response.

2) Generate a new etag that is derived from the object etag and something about grok. eg Etag: "<md5-of-grok-pattern>-<object-etag>". This will likely still cause an error in the swift CLI tool, but it gives clients that know about grok a better chance to use conditional requests with grok

issue_title: Unused stubs causes errors in subsequent tests
ResultSet iterators interfere with each other
issue_body:
If you create two or more iterators over a ResultSet, then they consume `ResultSet._rows` together, meaning they all iterate over different rows. I.e. something like:
```python
rows = db.query('select * from active_users')
itera = iter(rows)
iterb = iter(rows)
print(next(itera))  # row 1
print(next(iterb))  # row 2
```

Of course, once all rows have been iterated over and fully cached, then iterators behave correctly, since they are independently iterating over the `ResultSet._all_rows` list, something like:
```python
rows = db.query('select * from active_users')
for row in rows: continue
itera = iter(rows)
iterb = iter(rows)
print(next(itera))  # row 1
print(next(iterb))  # row 1
```

Maybe this is intentional, but to me it seems confusing / not a standard iterator behavior. :bus:

issue_title: schema validation duplicates reference types (dictionaries/lists)
issue_body:
I have a schema I am trying to validate which uses YAML's alias/anchor to prevent duplication of inputs. When trying to use `voluptuous` for validation on the YAML-parsed `dict`, it creates new instances for each alias. Can `voluptuous` be changed to understand visited items and not duplicate entries?

Example YAML (joke obviously):
```yaml
by month:
  November:
  - &mickey {birthday: '1928-11-18', name: Mickey}
by year:
  1928:
  - *mickey
````

Example test:

```python

import calendar
import datetime

def test_dict_reference():
    mouse = Schema({"name": str, "birthday": Date()})
    mice = Schema([mouse])
    schema = Schema({
        "by month": {In(set(calendar.month_name[1:])): mice},
        "by year": {Range(min=1900, max=datetime.datetime.now().year): mice}
    })
    mickey = dict(name="Mickey", birthday="1928-11-18")
    data = {"by month":
            {
                calendar.month_name[11]: [mickey]
            },
            "by year":
            {
                1928: [mickey],
            }
            }
    out = schema(data)
    orig = out['by month'][calendar.month_name[11]][0]
    dup = out['by year'][1928][0]
    assert orig is dup, 'they are not the same ({}) != ({})'.format(id(orig), id(dup))
```

issue_title: How to get type of output from OperatorArray?
issue_body:
I've run into a change from `formulas 0.0.7` to `0.0.10` which is causing me trouble. The result type no longer seems to respect the input type.

Old: multiplication on `int` returned `int`
```
$ pip list | grep formulas
formulas                 0.0.7    
$ python3
Python 3.6.2 |Continuum Analytics, Inc.| (default, Jul 20 2017, 13:14:59) 
[GCC 4.2.1 Compatible Apple LLVM 6.0 (clang-600.0.57)] on darwin
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> from formulas import Parser
>>> code = Parser().ast('=B1*2')[1].compile()
>>> out = code(10)
>>> out
20
>>> type(out)
<class 'int'>
>>> 
```

New: multiplication on `int` returns `OperatorArray`
```
$ pip list | grep formulas
formulas                 0.0.10    
$ python3
Python 3.6.2 |Continuum Analytics, Inc.| (default, Jul 20 2017, 13:14:59) 
[GCC 4.2.1 Compatible Apple LLVM 6.0 (clang-600.0.57)] on darwin
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> from formulas import Parser
>>> code = Parser().ast('=B1*2')[1].compile()
>>> out = code(10)
>>> out
OperatorArray(20.0, dtype=object)
>>> type(out)
<class 'formulas.functions.operators.OperatorArray'>
```

The output seems to be `OperatorArray` regardless of whether the actual output is int, float, or string. Is there any way to recover the result type from the OperatorArray?

Thanks for all your work on this project. We're very happy to be using it for [Workbench](https://github.com/CJWorkbench/cjworkbench).

issue_title: Migration 0003 is extremely slow
issue_body:
On my projects I currently can’t run migration 0003. It’s taking forever, I let it run more than 3 hours in vain. I’m using exclusively PostgreSQL.

That’s because of this query: https://github.com/etianen/django-reversion/blob/v2.0.0/reversion/migrations/0003_auto_20160601_1600.py#L15
It’s not returning distinct results because of [the default ordering of Version](https://github.com/etianen/django-reversion/blob/v2.0.0/reversion/models.py#L99). It’s therefore returning a gigantic list. This list is used to generate ludicrous SQL queries containing thousands of  `OR` (even millions in my case).

The solution is simply to add an empty `.order_by()` at the end of the line.

issue_title: Allow to configure columns reported
issue_body:
Referring again to the output of pytest-benchmark's test suite itself, I'd point out that it is 188 characters wide which is a lot more than 80 or 100.

In a lot of cases, you would just want some mix of mean/median/min/max; especially so in the manual mode when you know exactly what you are doing.

Like, I could look at the outliers, stddev and IQR while designing the benchmark, but when it's being reported at the end of the test suite run, I'll probably just look at min/mean.

With this in mind, it would be then nice if the report could be configured to include/exclude custom stat columns, like `stats=['min', 'mean'], sort='min'` (or command line parameters). Maybe the default setting should be not to output absolutely every stat that it computes, too.

issue_title: Would you consider package design which enables extraction of core functionality?
issue_body: I recently got interested in full-featured Test Impact Analysis in Python and I was thinking about creating a Python package [`python-tia`](https://github.com/fkromer/python-tia) which provides TIA functionality as new fun project. `pytest` is my Python test framework of choice as well. But I think TIA has a lot of potential which justifies the overhead of putting the core functionality of TIA into a separate package. This would enable integration of TIA with other test runners than `pytest`, other version control systems than `git` and probably even other coverage analysis tools than `coverage`. issue_title: iterselects break if not immediately used
issue_body:
Minimal example:
```
collection1 = db(db.table1.id > 0).iterselect(...)
collection2 = db(db.table2.id > 0).iterselect(...)
for row in collection1:
    do_something()
for row in collection2:
    do_something() # breaks
```

Root cause:
When the `iterselect` (`IterRows`) object is initialized, it is given a `gluon.contrib.pg8000.core.Cursor` attribute on `iter_rows._c`. At the same time, the attribute `iter_rows.db._adapter` is given the same `Cursor`.
However, since `db._adapter` only keeps track of the latest `Cursor`, after the iteration of `collection1`, the Cursor of `db._adapter` is closed - then `collection2` has no cursor anymore, and cannot execute the query.

issue_body:
See the issue reported here:
https://discuss.mxnet.io/t/problem-when-loading-saved-params/982/11

```python
self.model.load_params(self.model_path, ctx=ctx)
File “/home/name/virtualEnv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/mxnet/gluon/block.py”, line 317, in load_params
self.prefix)
File “/home/name/virtualEnv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/mxnet/gluon/parameter.py”, line 676, in load
self[name]._load_init(arg_dict[name], ctx)
File “/home/name/virtualEnv/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/mxnet/gluon/parameter.py”, line 209, in _load_init
assert set(ctx) == set(self._deferred_init[1]), 
IndexError: tuple index out of range
```

Code [here](https://github.com/ZhengzheYang/Reading-Comprehension/blob/19c591c767f7022730e8ec3aee6502f91f613266/model.py#L232)

Reproduce bug:
```python
embedding_size = 400
vocab_size = 100
batch_size = 1
ctx = mx.gpu()
​
# Create first model
test = RCv1(nd.ones((vocab_size, embedding_size)), vocab_size=vocab_size, batch_size=batch_size)
# Initialize params
test.model.collect_params().initialize(mx.init.Xavier(magnitude=2.24, rnd_type='gaussian'), ctx=ctx)
# Runs one batch
print('Model 1', test.model(nd.ones((batch_size, 25, 94, embedding_size), ctx), nd.ones((batch_size, 126), ctx)))
# Save params
test.model.save_params('test.params')
# Create second model
test2 = RCv1(nd.ones((vocab_size, embedding_size)), vocab_size=vocab_size, batch_size=batch_size)
# Load params
test2.model.load_params('test.params')
```
​
I was able to reproduce. Initializing the parameters from the newly created network prior to loading the parameters solved the issue. Couldn't figure out exactly what is wrong. I feel it could be related to the `embedding` layer being shared by the LSTM and CNN.

@piiswrong any ideas?

issue_title: How to use gluon to train GAN network on multiple GPUs?
issue_body:
I come up with a solution, but it trains discriminator for an epoch and then uses the same thing to train the generator.
```
def forward_backward(net, data, label):
    with autograd.record():
        losses_D = [loss(netD(X), Y) for X, Y in zip(data, label)]
    for l in losses_D:
        l.backward()

    with autograd.record():
        losses_G = [loss(netG(X), Y) for X, Y in zip(data, label)]
    for l in losses_G:
        l.backward()

label = gluon.utils.split_and_load(trainging_data, ctx)
forward_backward(net, data, label)
```
I want to update the both network by batch.  Something like:
```
def forward_backward(net, data, label):
    for X, Y in zip(data, label)
        with autograd.record():
            losses_D.append(loss(netD(X), Y))

        for l in losses_D:
            l.backward()

        with autograd.record():
            losses_G.append(loss(netG(X), Y))

        for l in losses_G:
            l.backward()

label = gluon.utils.split_and_load(trainging_data, ctx)
forward_backward(net, data, label)
```

I have no idea if this code will work. Please let me know if it works.

issue_title: Add to Audience with format_params doesn't respect LOOKALIKE_VALUE
issue_body:
When adding users to a lookalike value-based seed custom audience, one must pass a schema that contains the string `LOOKALIKE_VALUE`, like:
`[CustomAudience.Schema.MultiKeySchema.email, 'LOOKALIKE_VALUE''`.

https://developers.facebook.com/docs/marketing-api/value-based-lookalike-audience-targeting

And then users are added with that value passed as a numerical value, like an `int` or `float`.

The problem comes when a developer wishes to use the SDK to automatically format and hash params. The `format_params` function assumes that all values are strings when it runs:
```
key = key.strip(" \t\r\n\0\x0B.").lower()
key = CustomAudienceMixin.normalize_key(key_name, str(key))
```

The only workaround is to manually hash all values and invoke add with `pre_hashed=True`.

With a small enhancement, this package could make populating a value-based audience much simpler and allow end developers to use the formatting code already written.

issue_title: pycodestyle doesn't report issues if entry in setup.cfg exists
issue_body:
It looks like the pycodestyle plugin doesn't report any issue as soon as a configuration is made in the setup.cfg


The setup.cfg contains the following lines.

```
[flake8]
ignore = D100,D101,D104,E722
exclude = .git,__init__.py
max-line-length = 80

[mypy]
python_version = 3.3
silent_imports = True
ignore_errors = True
strict_optional = False

[pycodestyle]
ignore = E722
exclude = .git,__init__.py
max-line-length = 80

[pydocstyle]
ignore = D100,D101,D104
max-line-length = 80
```

### Expected behavior

All lines longer than 80 chars should be warned to be too long, but E722 (bare excepts) should be ignored.

### Actual behavior

With a `[pycodestyle]` section in the setup.cfg pycodestyle doesn't return any warnings/errors. Lines longer than 80 chars are accepted.

The debug log shows :

```
2017-10-24 19:20:11,635 UTC - DEBUG - pyls.plugins.pycodestyle_lint - Got pycodestyle config: {'verbose': 0, 'quiet': 0, 'repeat': True, 'exclude': '.git,__init__.py', 'filename': '*.py', 'select': '', 'ignore': 'E722', 'show_source': None, 'show_pep8': None, 'statistics': None, 'count': None, 'max_line_length': 80, 'hang_closing': None, 'format': 'default', 'diff': None, 'benchmark': None}
```

If the `[pycodestyle]` section is commented out both issues are recognized.

issue_title: NRML 0.5 format for the source model
issue_body:
Here is one proposal, which is mimicking what we did for the risk files in the migration NRML 0.4 -> 0.5:

- [x] we will keep backward compatibility forever, i.e. the engine will be able to read NRML 0.4 files and to upgrade them on-the-fly automatically and no warning will be printed
- [x] there will be a command `oq upgrade_nrml <dir>` to bulk-upgrade all the source models in the given directory
- [x] the new format will be the same as before, except that now the source model will be a sequence of SourceGroups containing sources instead of a sequence of sources
- [x] attributes of the SourceGroups will be transmitted to the underlying sources
- [x] the SourceGroup must have a mandatory attribute tectonicRegion
- [x] the number of srcs_weights is equal to the number of sources in the group and the sum of the
weights is one, if present.

Here is an example

```xml
<?xml version='1.0' encoding='utf-8'?>
<nrml xmlns:gml="http://www.opengis.net/gml"
      xmlns="http://openquake.org/xmlns/nrml/0.5">
  <sourceModel name="Test source model" grp_interdep='indep'>
    <sourceGroup name="group1"  tectonicRegion="Stable Continental Crust"
                 src_interdep="indep" rup_interdep="mutex"
                 srcs_weights="0.1 0.2 0.7">
      <pointSource id="1" name="point" >
        ...
      </pointSource>
      <pointSource id="2" name="point" >
        ...
      </pointSource>
      <pointSource id="3" name="point" >
        ...
      </pointSource>
    </sourceGroup>
    <sourceGroup name="group2"  tectonicRegion="Stable Continental Crust"
                 src_interdep="indep" rup_interdep="indep"
                 srcs_weights="0.1 0.2 0.7">
      <pointSource id="4" name="point">
        ...
      </pointSource>
      <pointSource id="5" name="point">
        ...
      </pointSource>
      <pointSource id="6" name="point">
        ...
      </pointSource>
    </sourceGroup>
        ...
  </sourceModel>
</nrml>
```

An alternative proposal is to make the parser smarter, so that it can read both NRML 0.4 and NRML 0.5, and also mixed files containing both sources and SourceGroups: in this case there will be no deprecation of the old format and no suggestion of upgrading it.

issue_title: [Docs] vim.host.VsanSystem UpdateVsan function name in docs is incorrect
issue_body:
There is a function listed on the [vim.host.VsanSystem](https://github.com/vmware/pyvmomi/blob/releases/docs/vim/host/VsanSystem.rst) documentation named `UpdateVsan`, but that function doesn't actually exist in the VsanSystem spec. It should be `UpdateVsan_Task`.

For example, I have a VsanSystem object (named vsan_sys) and a vsan.host.ConfigInfo object (named vsan_config) instantiated:
```
>>> type(vsan_sys)
<class 'pyVmomi.VmomiSupport.vim.host.VsanSystem'>
>>> type(vsan_config)
<class 'pyVmomi.VmomiSupport.vim.vsan.host.ConfigInfo'>
>>> vsan_sys
'vim.host.VsanSystem:vsanSystem'
```
However, I get an attributed error when calling `UpdateVsan`:
```
>>> vsan_sys.UpdateVsan(vsan_config)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
AttributeError: 'vim.host.VsanSystem' object has no attribute 'UpdateVsan'
```
After inspecting `dir`, it appears that the function's name is actually `UpdateVsan_Task`:
```
>>> dir(vsan_sys)
['AddDisks', 'AddDisks_Task', 'Array', 'InitializeDisks', 'InitializeDisks_Task', 'QueryDisksForVsan', 'QueryHostStatus', 'RemoveDisk', 'RemoveDiskMapping', 'RemoveDiskMapping_Task', 'RemoveDisk_Task', 'Update', 'UpdateVsan_Task', '_GetMethodInfo', '_GetMethodList', '_GetMoId', '_GetPropertyInfo', '_GetPropertyList', '_GetServerGuid', '_GetStub', '_InvokeAccessor', '_InvokeMethod', '__class__', '__delattr__', '__dict__', '__doc__', '__eq__', '__format__', '__getattribute__', '__hash__', '__init__', '__module__', '__new__', '__reduce__', '__reduce_ex__', '__repr__', '__setattr__', '__sizeof__', '__str__', '__subclasshook__', '__weakref__', '_methodInfo', '_moId', '_propInfo', '_propList', '_serverGuid', '_stub', '_version', '_wsdlName', 'config']
```
Which, after trying the different name, it works as expected (in this case, setting the `enabled` attribute):
```
>>> host_ref.config.vsanHostConfig.enabled
False
>>> vsan_sys.UpdateVsan_Task(vsan_config)
'vim.Task:haTask-ha-host-vim.host.VsanSystem.update-153924198'
>>> host_ref.config.vsanHostConfig.enabled
True
```

Hopefully if anyone runs into this one, this will help them instead of trying to figure out why this isn't working, at least until the docs can be amended.

Thanks!

issue_title: H5DataSet for Python backend
issue_body:
For an even cleaner separation between NIX classes and H5Py methods, we need a class that serves the same purpose that `H5Group` does, but for DataSets, called `H5DataSet`. Currently, to create a data set in the file, the `H5Group` class offers a *passthrough* method. Reading and writing data is done directly on the h5py object. An `H5DataSet` class will be implemented to handle these operations more cleanly.

To avoid potential confusion: The current `DataSet` class, which `DataArray` subclasses, does not serve this purpose exactly. The class does handle reading and writing data to the backend, but it is not used by other data objects, such as the labels of a `SetDimension` or the ticks of a `RangeDimension`. This class will be rewritten into `H5DataSet`.

issue_title: --pre behaves weirdly in various commands
issue_body:
Be sure to check the existing issues (both open and closed!).

Describe the issue briefly here.

- `pipenv lock` should respect `allow_prereleases` in existing Pipfile
- `pipenv install packagename` fails to pass `--pre` either from command line or from Pipfile into `pipenv lock`, causes the command to fail during locking
  - Either we pass `--pre` or have` allow_prereleases` in the Pipfile, as long as there's package name in the cli arguments, it does not work, because of this code fails to passing `pre=pre`, to my guess: https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/blob/master/pipenv/core.py#L2005
- `pipenv update --pre --outdated` not respecting the `--pre` flag, removing `--outdated` works

------------

##### Expected result

None of the things above works when there's a prerelease dependency in the Pipfile, for example, `proto-google-datastore-v1beta3`

##### Actual result

They should all work (IMO, at least for the last two)

##### Steps to replicate

Pipfile is as follows:

```
[[source]]
url = "https://pypi.python.org/simple"
verify_ssl = true
name = "pypi"

[packages]
"proto-google-datastore-v1beta3" = "*"

[dev-packages]
ipdb = "*"

[requires]
python_version = "3.6"

[pipenv]
allow_prereleases = true
```

Running any of the 3 commands above should fail with this Pipfile with the error

```
Warning: Your dependencies could not be resolved. You likely have a mismatch in your sub-dependencies.
  You can use $ pipenv install --skip-lock to bypass this mechanism, then run $ pipenv graph to inspect the situation.
Could not find a version that matches proto-google-datastore-v1beta3
Tried: 0.0.2rc0, 0.0.3rc0, 1.0.0b0, 1.0.0b1, 1.0.0b2
```

issue_title: `setuptools.extension.Extension` is old-style class
issue_body:
As of `setuptools == 28.7.0`, [`setuptools.extension.Extension`](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/blob/6d05b8dce90985455dce0649e04955985f336b04/setuptools/extension.py#L32) is an old-style class.
In Python 2.7, this raises an `AttributeError` with `cython == 0.25.1` when an `Extension` instance is passed to `collections.Iterable`, as described here: https://github.com/cython/cython/issues/1507.

I would like to ask whether this is intentional (I suspect that yes), and whether it can be avoided.
The root cause is that [`distutils.extension.Extension`](https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/ee324a8f516318e84914ebd18f3e8068faec14f9/Lib/distutils/extension.py#L26) is an old-style class.

issue_title: test command won't have dependencies on sys.path in subprocess
issue_body:
When running the `test` command, or any other command that invokes [with_project_on_sys_path](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/blob/master/setuptools/command/test.py#L114-L156), setuptools ensures those projects are on sys.path, but if a subprocess is launched that relies on those dependencies such as [tox does](https://github.com/tox-dev/tox/blob/d5d3f421f9a857caf872623162deb0c53e99bacc/tox/venv.py#L392) or any other project that launches subprocesses to create fixtures or launch services, that subprocess won't have the projects on sys.path so will fail, as reported in tox-dev/tox#360.

On project I maintain [works around this issue by adding all of sys.path to PYTHONPATH](https://github.com/yougov/pmxbot/blob/dd01624/tests/functional/__init__.py#L56-L58) for the subprocesses. Setuptools should do something similar (though more elegant by matching only those projects added), enabling this use case in general.

issue_title: Support transports without ports
issue_body:
Currently, Klein calls `request.getHost().port` (see https://github.com/twisted/klein/blob/master/src/klein/resource.py#L77-L81 ) to get the port number to add to the server name. Unfortunately, if the `IAddress` returned by `request.getHost()` doesn't have a `port` attribute (if it's from a loopback transport or a unix socket, for example) it breaks.

A possible solution is to check for a `port` attribute or catch an `AttributeError` instead of just assuming it's there. :-)

issue_title: Potential for out-of-range error to overwrite a user's reset_offsets instruction?
issue_body:
( @emmett9001 I got thinking about this while reviewing #265, but since I shouldn't be heaping multiple issues into the same thread as I've done in the past, here's a fresh ticket :))

I believe that in the usage model that @zsimpson describes in #253 -- where you'd start a new consumer, then immediately call `reset_offsets()` on it -- there's a special case where you might end up with the default `auto_offset_reset` value, rather than whatever you just passed to `reset_offsets()`. Here's the sequence of events (or the theory, anyway):

  * user starts a new consumer
  * in `start()`, `fetch_offsets()` runs, which happens to obtain a bunch of stale offsets (this is the special case -- maybe it's because the topic's just been compacted; whatever)
  * the fetch-worker starts, enters `fetch()`, grabs a bunch of fetch locks
  * user calls `reset_offsets()`
  * `reset_offsets()` blocks trying to get a lock that the fetch-worker holds
  * the fetch-worker gets a response with `OffsetOutOfRangeError` on all partitions (not necessarily all, but that's the easiest case to reason about)
  * the fetch-worker then [releases locks on those partitions][0]
  * this means the user's `reset_offsets()` call can now grab those locks, and do its work
  * the fetch-worker waits for that to finish, then [continues with its own resets][1]
  * end result: the user-provided offsets got clobbered

I suspect it's really hard to get a test case written that demonstrates this reliably, but if we're happy to merge a fix without that test case, I'd think not releasing the locks before resetting, and instead using an `RLock` should do the trick. Happy to take a shot at that.

[0]: https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/770c4fc114dcb4ba0752240c81a56ae717ae50ab/pykafka/simpleconsumer.py#L619-L624
[1]: https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/770c4fc114dcb4ba0752240c81a56ae717ae50ab/pykafka/simpleconsumer.py#L627-L630

issue_title: Tools for calibrating photutils outputs
issue_body:
Prompted by the question in #433 (although this has been discussed in various other places), we should develop some functions to do standard sorts of calibrations given the outputs of `photutil`'s aperture and PSF photometry elements.

The way I imagine this working is basically a set of functions that take tables as they are output from the aperture photometry routines or PSF photometry classes, applying various calibrations.  The minimal example is something like this:
```python
def calibrate_magnitudes(tab, zeropoint):
    tab['cal_mags'] = -2.5*np.log10(tab['flux_fit'])*u.mag + zeropoint
```

But a much wider set of tools are called for, including:
* Take an output table with `SkyCoord`s, and use that to match some standard star catalogs (which might or might not be provided in the package), and use that to estimate photometric zero points
* Use bright stars to do aperture corrections from flux measurements
* Combining *multiple* catalogs from different exposures, matching up objects that are likely the same, and computing the combined magnitudes/uncertainties

These are all quite distinct tasks, but I think we want all of them, at least at some point.

issue_title: `test_wrap_around` sometimes fails on python 3.4
issue_body:
## Description
In developing #3072, I found that `test_wrap_around.py` **sometimes** fail on python3.4.
I've been trying to [reproduce this](https://travis-ci.org/hmaarrfk/scikit-image/jobs/379259181#L5365), but it is generally difficult.

```bash
tests/test_unwrap.py::test_wrap_around[2-0] FAILED                                     [ 83%]
tests/test_unwrap.py::test_wrap_around[2-1] PASSED                                   [ 84%]
tests/test_unwrap.py::test_wrap_around[3-0] PASSED                                   [ 86%]
tests/test_unwrap.py::test_wrap_around[3-1] PASSED                                   [ 88%]
tests/test_unwrap.py::test_wrap_around[3-2] PASSED 
```

## Way to reproduce
The easiest way is probably to use [my branch](https://github.com/hmaarrfk/scikit-image/tree/bugfix_pass_test) since it passes many other tests than the one currently on scikit-image.
Specifically you should use `bugfix_pass_test_dont_skip_test_wrap_around` which actually runs the test instead of skipping it in `python 3.4`
```bash
git clone git@github.com:hmaarrfk/scikit-image.git scikit-image-34
cd scikit-image-34
git checkout bugfix_pass_test_dont_skip_test_wrap_around
pipenv --python 3.4
pipenv install --skip-lock -r 'requirements/test.txt'
pipenv install --skip-lock -r 'requirements/build.txt'
pipenv install --skip-lock -r 'requirements/docs.txt'
pipenv run python setup develop.py
pipenv run pytest -v
```
To specifically run the test that fails
```bash
pipenv run pytest -v skimage/restoration/tests/test_unwrap.py
```
and just press "up arrow"+"enter" a few times until the test fails.

issue_title: allow tifffile.imsave to handle file/io objects
issue_body:
Currently, image saving in `sklearn.external.tifffile.imsave` only allows the user to supply the name of the file to which the image will be written. This precludes the possibility of writing to more general IO objects -- e.g. byte streams.

I would be interested in putting together a PR that allows the user to supply either a string or a file/io object to be written to, but I wanted to gauge interest and get some feedback before diving in.

issue_title: The value of circularity calculated from regionprops of skimage
issue_body: I use `[(4 * math.pi * region.area) / (region.perimeter * region.perimeter) for region in regionprops(label_image)]` to calculate the circularity of each labeled region in `label_image`. The value of that expression is large than 1. But the value of circularity must be (0, 1].

I have not exactly undestand why, but tests fails when try to create a new user, because PostgreSQL try to assign id = 1 (that already exists in database).
I found a solution moving [this code](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/blob/master/invenio/ext/legacy/__init__.py#L50-L54) inside a fixture.

issue_title: WebSubmit: `pdfopt` called even when not available in `CFG_PATH_PDFOPT`
issue_labels: [{'id': 96318385, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio/labels/Branch:%20maint-1.0', 'name': 'Branch: maint-1.0', 'color': 'd4c5f9', 'default': False}, {'id': 96238698, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio/labels/Component:%20WebSubmit', 'name': 'Component: WebSubmit', 'color': 'ededed', 'default': False}, {'id': 96237777, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio/labels/Type:%20bug', 'name': 'Type: bug', 'color': 'ff2a00', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
Originally on 2013-07-18

PDF can be _linearized_ (for fast web viewing) using `pdfopt` in several steps of the conversion chain handled by `websubmit_file_converter.py`. For eg. the following functions would explicitly call `CFG_PATH_PDFOPT`:
- `pdf2pdfx(…, pdfopt=False, …)`
- `pdf2pdfa(…, pdfopt=True, …)`
- `pdf2pdfopt(…)`
- `ps2pdfx(…, pdfopt=False, …)`
- `ps2pdfa(…, pdfopt=True, …)`
- `ps2pdf(…, pdfopt=True, …)`
  in a configurable manner (with `pdfopt` parameter, mostly set by default to `True`). In the conversion chain handled by `convert_file(…)` the above functions would be called with their default value for `pdfopt` parameter, independently of the availability of the `pdfopt` path in `CFG_PATH_PDFOPT`. It would be good to update the conversion chain (in `get_conversion_map()`) to use `{'pdfopt': can_pdfopt()}` as parameter for each of the above functions with `pdfopt=True`, or/and call `can_pdfopt()` within all the above functions prior to calling `CFG_PATH_PDFOPT`.

Note that until recently `pdfopt` would _in principle_ be available when `ghostcript` exists (such that _in principle_ the above functions would not be called when `pdfopt` does not exist) but `pdfopt` [has recently been removed](http://git.ghostscript.com/?p=ghostpdl.git;a=commitdiff;h=260ddb3e53ffe852a8994dd00719931ca3f2beec) from `Ghostscript` package. See also #1561. One could imagine other cases where `pdfopt` is not found at install/configuration time while `Ghostscript` is.

issue_title: bibupload doesn't respect --continue-on-error
issue_body: I'm running invenio 1.2.1 and bibupload stops the bibsched queue even though it was run with --continue-on-error. I've set to CFG_BIBSCHED_NEVER_STOPS to 1 in order to bypass this issue for now.

issue_title: views: bulk support
issue_body: When the REST API is used to upload thousands of records it would be more efficient to do this in bulk to avoid the overhead of thousands of HTTP request. Using elasticsearch REST API as model, something like a ``POST <prefix>/_bulk`` endpoint would probably be the best way instead of overloading the ``POST <prefix>/`` endpoint.

issue_title: W/o setuptools, "setup.py install" skips zope/interface/common/
issue_body:
In https://bugs.launchpad.net/zope.interface/+bug/611362, @exarkun reported:

> After installing the 3.6.1 release from PyPI, the zope.interface.common package is not available:

    exarkun@boson:~/Downloads$ tar xzf zope.interface-3.6.1.tar.gz
    exarkun@boson:~/Downloads$ cd zope.interface-3.6.1/
    exarkun@boson:~/Downloads/zope.interface-3.6.1$ python setup.py install --quiet --prefix /tmp/ZOPE-TEST
    /usr/lib/python2.6/distutils/dist.py:266: UserWarning: Unknown distribution option: 'test_suite'
    ....
    exarkun@boson:~/Downloads/zope.interface-3.6.1$ PYTHONPATH=/tmp/ZOPE-TEST/ python -c 'from zope.interface import common'
    Traceback (most recent call last):
        File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
    ImportError: cannot import name common
    exarkun@boson:~/Downloads/zope.interface-3.6.1$

and supplied a patch:

  https://bugs.launchpad.net/zope.interface/+bug/611362/+attachment/1471651/+files/setup.patch

I followed up:

> Hmm, this seems to be true only when installing without setuptools:

@glyph followed up:

> I just heard about a case of this happening on Ubuntu 12.10. I suspect it's a bug in setuptools or distutils or ubuntu's patching of same, not zope.interface; can someone comment on what mechanism ZI uses to include the 'common' package? I don't see it in setup.cfg, or a manifest file... I am guessing there's some setuptools magic which is supposed to make it get included.
> Also of note; setuptools *was* installed on the system where this happened. It was zope.interface 4.0.3 in this case.

issue_title: Issue with upcoming release of SQLAlchemy 1.1
issue_body:
Zope.sqlalchemy is calling `session.transaction._iterate_parents()` in [datamanger.py](https://github.com/zopefoundation/zope.sqlalchemy/blob/88fc88370a909822c14860a86ce7ad6b87109fc4/src/zope/sqlalchemy/datamanager.py#L65). Unfortunately, this method has been renamed to `_iterate_self_and_parents()` in SQLAlchemy 1.1 ([see here](https://github.com/zzzeek/sqlalchemy/commit/0a29071b16e1844b5e1d71aead0e8a1ae83b48d0)).

So this should be fixed somehow or things will break when SQLAlchemy 1.1 will be release (soon).

issue_title: Significance of         **self.device_serial = None**  Statement
issue_body:
class StateConnection(object):
    """ A class controlling the connection state of a device. """

    TYPE_USB = 0
    TYPE_REMOTE = 1

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        """
            Init a new connection state, defaulting to a USB
            connection.
        """

        self.usb = True
        self.network = False
        self.host = '127.0.0.1'
        self.port = 27042
        self._type = self.TYPE_USB
        **self.device_serial = None**



What is significance of this  device_serial in this class and why this is none


Thanks

Bikee Bihari

issue_title: Transition condition can't access User obejct
issue_body:
In order to allow certain transitions only to a set of staff users, I'd like to access the User object inside my customized condition functions.

This unfortunately is not possible, because that function never "sees" the ``request`` object. By patching the templatetag function ``fsm_transition_hints`` and the method ``FSMTransitionMixin.get_transition_hints()`` is would easily by possible to pass to ``request`` object to my condition function.

If you have concerns about backward compatibility with existing condition functions, I could add a boolean named, say ``pass_request`` to the ``custom`` dict. Then the ``request`` object is passed in only when this is ``True``. 

What do you think about this and shall I create a pull request for this feature?

issue_title: Signals for each action
issue_body: It would be nice to be able to receive signals for each of the operations that is handled by djoser. This way the app that is using djoser can do logging of what is happening. For example a user reset their password. DJoser doesnt do logging by itself, so it would be good for the app to log this in the server logs (for debugging purposes).

issue_title: `JWT_ISSUER` Not included in token encode
issue_body:
I followed the instructions in the docs setting up an end point for auth and another for token verification and also set the `JWT_ISSUER` setting. I was confused to find that, upon receiving a token and turning around and trying to verify it with the other endpoint that it failed due to a missing issuer exception. I dug into the source and saw that this is apparently intentional and in the unit tests the issuer is set manually after the `jwt_payload_handler` function is run:

https://github.com/GetBlimp/django-rest-framework-jwt/blob/master/tests/test_utils.py#L132

I'm fairly new to JWT so I might be misunderstanding something, but shouldn't the `iss` key with the `JWT_ISSUER` setting value be set in the encode step? I'm happy to make a pull request if that is the case. Thanks!

issue_title: Installing buildozer for Python 3 failed
issue_body:
I tried to install buildozer with Python 3 support as per the instructions here: 

https://pypi.python.org/pypi/buildozer

which are 

> Installing Buildozer with python3 support:
> 
> The pip package does not yet support python3.
> 
>     Install buildozer from source:
> 
>     git clone https://github.com/kivy/buildozer
>     cd buildozer
>     python setup.py build
>     sudo pip install -e .

but the last step (sudo pip install -e .) fails.  

I'm using XUbuntu 16.04.  The output of the commands is below.  Any help would be greatly appreciated.

issue_title: QHeaderView.setSectionResizeMode

issue_title: Snippet client's _is_app_running() doesn't work properly, leading to race conditions
issue_body:
Slow loading snippets fail because _is_app_running() returns True even when nothing is on the other side of the socket.
This leads to race conditions between the snippet app really starting and the connection attempt.

```
>>> sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
>>> print self._port
8822
>>> result = sock.connect_ex(('127.0.0.1', self._port))
>>> print result
0
And yet...
$ nc 127.0.0.1 8822
$
```

issue_title: "integrity checks" should throw exceptions
issue_body:
Currently, the signature of an integrity check call looks like
```c++
bool verify_integrity(IntegrityCheckContext&, const std::string& name) const;
```

This is very cumbersome to use: instead we should simply have
```c++
void verify_integrity(const std::string& name) const;
```
The call would then throw an exception upon encountering an error.

In Python, we will replace `assert df.internal.check()` with simply `df.internal.check()`.
Just so we don't forget, the next release should have the copyright notice upgraded to 2016.  Usually we do this on master after all the branches for that release have been merged in.

issue_title: Test helpers for operations
issue_body:
### Description

Would be nice to be able to have some helper functions to test the operations of the API not depending on the paths, but on the `operationId`. Why? Because since you care more about the code when implementing the API you just have to remember the `operationId` which is the code path to the function/method you want to test. You don't want to test the URL path, this is guaranteed by Connexion that the path will be the one you defined in the specification.

Flask have [`url_for`](http://flask.pocoo.org/docs/0.11/api/#flask.url_for), but it needs a lot of boilerplate to use in tests (outside an application context).

### Expected behaviour

Maybe something reusing the `url_for` of Flask itself. It should be straight forward, something like:

```python
def test_some_operation(api_client, test_node_id):
    url_path_to_my_operation = connexion.url_for('my_api.service_node_users',
                                                 {'node_id': test_node_id})
    response = api_client.get(path_to_my_operation)
    assert response.status_code == 200
```

```yaml
  /service_nodes/{node_id}/users:
    parameters:
        - name: node_id
           in: path
           type: string
    get:
      summary: List users for a Service Node.
      operationId: myapi.service_node_users
      responses:
        200:
          description: OK
```

issue_title: Not getting changed value of class attribute in deserialized version
issue_body:
When I change a class variable defined in a module (in a file), I'm still getting the old value upon deserializing. This seems inconsistent when compared to dilling an interactively declared class, where the variable does indeed reflect the changed value. 

Say we define a class in file ```A.py```:
```
class A:
   b = 1
```
In **first** Python shell I serialize the changed class:
```
>>> from A import A
>>> A.b
1
>>> A.b = 42
>>> A.b
42
>>> with open('A.pkl', 'w') as f: dill.dump(A, f)
... 
```
In a **second** Python shell we deserialize, however the class variable retains the original value:
```
>>> with open('A.pkl', 'r') as f: A = dill.load(f)
... 
>>> A.b
1
```
Interestingly enough, everything works as expected so long as I define my class interactively. For example, in **first** Python shell we can do:
```
>>> class A:
...   b = 1
... 
>>> A.b
1
>>> A.b = 42
>>> A.b
42
>>> with open('A.pkl', 'w') as f: dill.dump(A, f)
...
```
Then in **second** Python shell, the result is as expected:
```
>>> with open('A.pkl', 'r') as f: A = dill.load(f)
...
>>> A.b
42
```

issue_title: Custom panic handler wipes out backtrace rendering
issue_body:
Inserting a panic in the native engine on master, you will see:
```
$ ./pants list 3rdparty/:
CRITICAL] panic at 'not yet implemented', src/rust/engine/src/scheduler.rs:40
CRITICAL] Please file a bug at https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/issues.
fatal runtime error: failed to initiate panic, error 5
Fatal Python error: Aborted
```

...due to the custom panic handler installed by https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/7319b21030f020ffefabebda30e5f6fbf48cf248/src/python/pants/engine/native.py#L721

But, annoyingly, this custom panic handler disables the highly useful `RUST_BACKTRACE` facility. Disabling the panic handler by commenting out its installation, I see:
```
thread '<unnamed>' panicked at 'not yet implemented', src/rust/engine/src/scheduler.rs:40
note: Run with `RUST_BACKTRACE=1` for a backtrace.
fatal runtime error: failed to initiate panic, error 5
Fatal Python error: Aborted
```
... and when I pass `RUST_BACKTRACE=1`, I get an actual useful backtrace pointing to the location of the panic.

issue_title: Pants lint engine only supports Python 2
issue_body:
The pants lint engine in the `contrib` directory only supports Python 2 linting at the moment, even if lint plugins are capable of supporting static analysis on Python 3 ASTs. This is due to the lint engine using the Python 2 stdlib `ast` module to parse the source code for plugin consumption. Since `ast` cannot parse code that is not compatible with the interpreter that invokes `ast.parse`, this inherently blocks other Python versions from being able to parse and lint properly.

The scope of this ticket is to investigate Python code that is incompatible with the interpreter running Pants.

issue_title: Add a Task 'implementation version', to allow Task implementers to invalidate artifacts
issue_body:
The implementation version would be a (string) property of a Task that a Task implementer could use to invalidate all artifacts for the Task.

Additionally, in the case of `Task.incremental==True`, the implementation version should be used to skip incrementalism. The easiest way to do this would likely be to include the current implementation version in the `results_dir` path, and to _not_ persist it in the build invalidator.

issue_title: satosa-saml-metadata creates <EntitiesDescriptor> for a single entity
issue_body:
I got a report that satosa-saml-metadata always creates the root element <EntitiesDescriptor> even if there is only one Entity. This apparently breaks the metadata for some implementations.

https://docs.oasis-open.org/security/saml/v2.0/saml-metadata-2.0-os.pdf
2.3 Root Elements A SAML metadata instance describes either a single entity or multiple entities. In the former case, the root element MUST be <EntityDescriptor> In the latter case, the root element MUST be <EntitiesDescriptor>

issue_title: HTTP request helper parses response as JSON without checking for errors
issue_body:
The `http_request` helper method in `requester.py` unconditionally attempts to parse the response as JSON: https://github.com/plaid/plaid-python/blob/02db6f522b9381361272746123ac916616d48674/plaid/requester.py#L33 By "unconditionally" I mean that it doesn't first check the HTTP response status code, nor is the `json.loads` call wrapped in a try/except block that would allow it to handle failures. This means that if you get a response like the following, you get a `JSONDecodeError`: ```html 502 Bad Gateway

502 Bad Gateway

nginx
``` Ideally, the method should either/both check the HTTP status code and/or catch exceptions from `json.loads`, and raise a custom exception as appropriate, without leaking the `JSONDecodeError`.

issue_title: Add a proxy flag to customize URLs for rewriting
If an ipfs hash does not resolve, the replay system waits a long time before timing out
issue_body:
We previously used a timeout on the IPFS_API.cat command but that was causing some problems relating to premature time out. A user will wait a long time with no feedback from the replay UI if the hash cannot be resolved. Example:

1. Create a CDXJ from a newly created WARC or other contents using ipwb index.
2. Copy the cdxj to a new machine.
3. Completely remove the ~/.ipfs dir from the original machine (it's the only way to be sure).
4. Replay the CDXJ on the second machine.
5. Note that ipwb replay shows that the memento is available (we need to change this verbiage) but clicking on a URI-M causes a very long delay until the browser times out.

We may want to integrate with js-ipfs to check if the payload is actually available. We may also be able to do this with the current system.

issue_title: Helper shortcuts for navigating in a given folder
issue_body:
right now, something like grabbing all the bed files in a given folder requires some roundabout logic of going down to the vault level and then searching with the folder path

```
folder = Object.retrieve(folder_id)
folder.parent.vault.files(path=folder.path + '.*bed.gz')
folder.vault.files(path=folder.path + '.*bed.gz')
```

it'd be awesome if I could just... Object.retrieve(folder_id).objects() or Object.retrieve(folder_id).files(path='.*csv') or something. it's also more intuitive.

issue_title: Add L2VPN/EVPN to next-hop sizing check to fix L2VPN/EVPN peers
issue_body:
##### ISSUE TYPE
 - Bug Report


##### SUMMARY

Commit 4aed9cbf81cbe544253e7da7c97feeb238bfea43 added next-hop sizing checks but did not add the sizing information for L2VPN / EVPN address-family.  This causes L2VPN/EVPN peers to be torn down.

##### STEPS TO REPRODUCE

Connect to a L2VPN/EVPN neighbor and watch the session come down.

##### FIX

I corrected this with just a quick add to the Family class - but given the rest of the changes in the commit there may be other areas that need some attention for EVPN.

Without this - an EVPN peer gets torn down (with an unsupported message being sent) , after this adjustment the peer is stable and passing information.

```
diff --git a/lib/exabgp/protocol/family.py b/lib/exabgp/protocol/family.py
index 62b90a3..2747811 100644
--- a/lib/exabgp/protocol/family.py
+++ b/lib/exabgp/protocol/family.py
@@ -264,6 +264,7 @@ class Family (object):
                (AFI.ipv6,SAFI.flow_ip):   ((0,16,32), 0),
                (AFI.ipv6,SAFI.flow_vpn):  ((0,16,32), 0),
                (AFI.l2vpn,SAFI.vpls):     ((4,),      0),
+               (AFI.l2vpn,SAFI.evpn):     ((4,),      0),
                (AFI.bgpls,SAFI.bgp_ls):   ((4,),      0),
        }
```

(for small items like this I just copy n paste - but if a full merge req. is preferred - just let me know)

issue_title: How to configure serving Demo offline?
issue_body:
This is a great project, so a huge thank you to your organisation! I've been experimenting with the demo recently, as per the instructions here: https://github.com/allenai/allennlp/blob/master/tutorials/getting_started/installation.md

I used pip installation instructions and was able to get around the pip/demo building issue by building with npm from the relevant folder cloned from github.

My problem is that, even once the demo has done the initial download of the model files from S3, it still can't be run offline because it appears to retry the download every time. Is there a configuration file I can change to point to the now downloaded files? Ideally it would check the file presence first (like the maybe_download feature in tensorflow)

If you need further details from me, let me know (I'm away from the computer in question but could include the error output if it's needed but I'm hoping the config will fix if I can just find where it's set 🙂)

Many thanks,
Neil

issue_title: Bug when starting a service with name that is the start of an already running service
issue_body:
The latest release has introduced a problem that means a service will fail to start if there is a service whose name starts with the new services name.

EG. If you have 2 services GG and GG_STUBS and you declare them in a profile as 
```
GG_STUBS GG
```
then GG will fail to start. 

Switching the order and both will start.

issue_title: Bring back `--touch` option to DSC interface
issue_body:
RE point 2 in issue #133: "touch" means to ignore changes in script and force everything up to date without rerun. As discussed in #133, a safe `--touch` is very difficult because the way file are named and their signatures handled in DSC. That is the reason I initially implemented a `--touch` option yet left it out the command interface: not removed, just do not show in the help message.

However I do use it myself quite often, because it is really a pain to make cosmetic changes to a computational intensive script and trigger a rerun. I can imaging how irritating it is for other DSC users who cannot see this possibility. So I brought back `--touch` to the command interface in current `master.` It reads:

```
  --touch          "Touch" output files if exist, to mark them "up-to-
                        date". It will override "--skip" option. Note that
                        time stamp is irrelevant to whether or not a file is
                        up-to-date. Files will be considered to "exist" as
                        long as module name, module parameters and variables,
                        module script name and command arguments remain the
                        same. Module script content do not matter. The output
                        files will be "touched" to match with the current
                        status of module code. (default: False)
```

Again, this is not "bullet proof"!! (for reasons in #133 which are too difficult to explain it fully even to internal users). However, as long as the `result/*.map.mpk` file (a DSC meta data file) presents and is up-to-date, the `--touch` option should be safe to "touch" everything to the state indicated by this meta-data file. I now added a check for presence of this file before applying `--touch`. So we are almost safe. It can still be messed up but it really will take some skills. I can double check it by creating a file signature for this meta file and only touch it if this signature matches, but it makes it harder to port a DSC from one computer to another without rerunning; and it still is not bullet proof ... so I guess I'll live without double-checking.

issue_title: Named module instances
issue_body:
Related to #111, we can add names to parameters to specify "scenarios", for example,

```
simulate: ...
  (n,p): large_p = (100, 150), large_p = (100, 1000), small_p = (100, 10)
```

Now challenges, similar to #111, is : 

1. We need a syntax on DSC level
2. We need to think about query, possibly such as `simulate.n.tag` to query by tag vs `simulate.n` to query by the value.

issue_title: get-identities: output only identity id or name
issue_body:
get-identities does not appear to have an easy way to get back just the identity id for a username.  For example, what I want is:

$ globus get-identities tuecke@uchicago.edu --xxx
b04b1e76-d274-11e5-bf36-07c9790e3d20

Perhaps --xxx should be "--format short".  Or "--fields identity_id --format plain".  

Likewise

tuecke@uchicago.edu

For multi-valued input, have one output per line, the same order as they appear on the command line.  For example:

globus get-identities tuecke@uchicago.edu tuecke@globusid.org --xxx
b04b1e76-d274-11e5-bf36-07c9790e3d20
b0497184-d274-11e5-bf33-130e800f88c2

issue_title: Move configuration for non production Globus environments into the SDK?
issue_body:
In a recent discussions about our deployment processes, we raised the possibility of testing the SDK against non-production Globus resources (i.e. internal prerelease services).

On a related note, the SDK defines tooling to make it possible to dispatch to non production services, as it must, in an environment variable.

The question that I'd raise is this: should we stop dancing around this issue and make the requisite configuration part of the SDK itself? It makes our lives easier, but publicly exposes some non-public assets.

My gut call is that the trade-off is worth it.

issue_title: non ASCII compatible UTF-8 inputs break on python 2.7
issue_body:
When looking into / writing tests for https://github.com/globus/globus-cli/issues/65, I ran into some `UnicodeEncodeError: 'ascii' codec can't encode characters` errors from the SDK when using python 2.7

It seems that python 2.7 breaks when it tries to use the default ascii codec to deal with the type inconsistency between string literals being byte strings and `safe_stringify` always returning unicode.

The simplest solution I could think of was to import `unicode_literals` from `__future__` in modules that use `safe_stringify` to make the necessary internal strings be unicode regardless of python version, but was wondering if there was a better solution? I think `safe_stringify` was originally intended to output byte strings for python 2, which might also solve this problem?

issue_title: SMC integration with py-EVM
issue_body:
Right now, the `ShardSyncer` passively listens to their peers for new collations and downloads all of those. In a real network however, it should check the SMC for headers and specifically download the bodies for those. To this end, we need a way to interact with the SMC.

In the sharding repository we have the classes `ShardTracker` and `SMCHandler` that provide an interface for accessing the SMC. We need a service that uses those and bridges to the `ShardSyncer`. Here's how I imagine it to look like:

### `class SMCService`

* Has an `SMCHandler`
* Has a `ShardTracker`
* Periodically (at least once per block) triggers the `ShardTracker` for new logs
* Processes those logs to keep an always up-to-date view of relevant part of the SMC state
* Other services can register queues to receive headers whenever they are added or confirmed (`register_added_header_queue`, `register_confirmed_header_queue`)
* Provides a method to add headers (mostly pass-through method `SMCHandler`)

For notaries `SMCService` is subclassed:

### `class NotaryService(SMCService)`

* Has a private key
* Provides events `registered`, `deregistered` and `released`
* Provides a method `submit_vote` (mostly pass-through method `SMCHandler`)

The shard syncer will

* Register to the `register_added_header_queue` and/or `register_confirmed_header_queue`
* Add those headers to the shard
* Wait for a corresponding `NewCollationHashes` message if the header is relatively new (say, half a period)
* Request the collation body otherwise
* When receiving a body vote for it if possible

Changes required at `ShardTracker`: As far as I can see only a way to get all header-added and notary-voted logs from the SMC for all of history so that the `ShardSyncer` can sync from genesis.

@hwwhww @NIC619

issue_title: Standardize trailing/preceding slashes
issue_body:
I'm not sure if this issue belongs here or elsewhere; I'm happy to move it if wanted.

A quick look at https://github.com/taskcluster/taskcluster-client.py/blob/master/apis.json shows that the current `baseUrl`s in the api appear to haphazardly include or exclude a trailing slash, which resulted in a `buildUrl` call returning a double slash in a url, which broke the tree.

I created https://github.com/taskcluster/taskcluster-client.py/pull/112 to address the issue; if we continue to sanitize all `baseUrl`s and `route`s before joining, we should be good going forward. However, it would likely be even better if there were a standard: either all `baseUrl`s should have a trailing slash, or all `baseUrl`s shouldn't have a trailing slash. We could enforce this in the api.js generation, in the schema, or run tests that fail when the wrong thing happens.

issue_title: Show python version in conda environment list
issue_body: It would be nice if the `conda info -e` and `conda env list` commands also showed what version of python was used in the virtual environment. I have a lot of environments and sometimes it's hard to remember which python version is in each environment without activating the env and running `python --version`.

issue_title: Inconsistency in meta data `icon` field
issue_body:
There are multiple different things that the `icon` could be:
- An md5 filename (local packages)
- Full remote URL to a file (repo data)
- Local installed packages have `icondata` which is a base64 field of the contents of the data

Currently I have to check multiple things to simply display an image:
- Is `icondata` not null?  Display base64-embedded image
- Is `icon` present and contains `://`?  Assume it references full URL and display without change
- Is `icon` present without `://`?  Assume it references a filename and prefix with base URL

There needs to be some form of consistency here.

issue_title: Conda multiple libpng versions
issue_body:
I have a conda environment with all `anaconda` packages. Recently, I explored the idea to install the `IRKernel` in my environment:

`conda install -c r r-irkernel`

```
The following NEW packages will be INSTALLED:

    r-irkernel: 0.3-r3.1.3_0

The following packages will be UPDATED:

    libpng:     1.5.13-1 --> 1.6.17-0
```

Updating `libpng` is problematic for me, since it would break all packages that are still linked to the old `libpng` library.

Is there any workaround for that? Can I somehow keep multiple `libpng` versions around?

issue_title: provide a way to update packages installed with pip
issue_body:
As known there are packages not hosted in conda. These are installed by pip. On the other hand there is no known way to update them easily. conda update --all does not do the job.

Is there a better way then described at https://stackoverflow.com/questions/33187676/update-all-pip-packages-that-dont-come-from-conda ?

issue_title: Conda 4.2 branch doesn't support CIO_TEST.
issue_body:
In `conda 4.1.x`, in config.py I see:

```
def get_channel_urls(platform=None):
    if os.getenv('CIO_TEST'):
        import cio_test
        base_urls = cio_test.base_urls
```

cio_test is not imported anywhere in 4.2.

issue_title: python 3.5 not possible with numpy 1.9?
issue_body:
Due to https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/6467 I need to pin my `numpy` to 1.9.*
I noticed today, that that prevents me from having a full suite of python3.5 apps:
```bash
(py35)
klay6683 at macd2860 in ~
$ conda install --yes pandas matplotlib blaze dask scikit-learn scikit-image gdal
Fetching package metadata: ....
Solving package specifications: .........................
Error: Unsatisfiable package specifications.
Generating hint:
[      COMPLETE      ]|#########################################################################| 100%


Hint: the following packages conflict with each other:
  - blaze
  - python 3.5*
```
My python 3.4 and 2.7 envs had no problem with the numpy pinning so far. (even so the openssl package is dangling back and forth between `1.0.2d-0` and `1.0.1k-1`, depending on what is being installed.

Is it possible to get things complete for python 3.5?

issue_title: Enable Fused Types in Cython with Carray
issue_body:
I love bcolz. It's so useful I made an extension for it (thanks to the [bquery](https://github.com/visualfabriq/bquery) guys, for blazing that trail). Unfortunately, while I was making the extension I ran into a problem. Cython's method for doing templating ([Fused Types](http://docs.cython.org/src/userguide/fusedtypes.html)) doesn't seem to be supported by `bcolz.carray`.

This is the hack that I created to get around that (note the third argument `type_indicator`):
https://github.com/pinleague/bdot/blob/master/bdot/carray_ext.pyx#L90

It uses `numpy`'s support for fused types to solve the problem. 

Thanks for all the hard work you've already done making such an awesome library. Adding this extra bit to carray would give extension writers an obvious solution to the templating problem and make extension writing a bit easier.

Here are some possibly helpful resources for anyone who wants to tackle the problem:

* [Python Buffer Protocol](https://docs.python.org/3/c-api/buffer.html)
* [Typed Memoryviews](http://docs.cython.org/src/userguide/memoryviews.html)

issue_title: Would it be possible for Curator to cache its index metadata over chained tasks in a task file?
issue_body:
On very large clusters, it can take a few minutes for curator to generate its index metadata. I noticed in the debug logs that these two steps in particular took a very long time:

```
2016-10-26 16:14:12,504 DEBUG          curator.indexlist          _get_metadata:145  Getting index metadata
2016-10-26 16:14:12,504 DEBUG          curator.indexlist       empty_list_check:182  Checking for empty list
```

There is an obvious inefficiency here when your realize that my first task


```
  1:
    action: close
    description: >-
      close indices. We do this first to remove heap pressure on the warm nodes.
    options:
      continue_if_exception: True
      ignore_empty_list: True
      delete_aliases: True
      timeout_override: 600
    filters:
      - filtertype: pattern
        kind: prefix
        value: 'logstash-'
        exclude: False
      - filtertype: age
        source: creation_date
        direction: older
        unit: days
        unit_count: 30
        exclude: False
  2:
    action: delete_indices
    description: >-
      delete indices. We do this early on to free up space on the warm nodes
    options:
      continue_if_exception: True
      ignore_empty_list: True
      timeout_override: 600
    filters:
      - filtertype: pattern
        kind: prefix
        value: 'logstash-'
        exclude: False
      - filtertype: age
        source: creation_date
        direction: older
        unit: days
        unit_count: 90
        exclude: False
```

yielded an empty list; the second necessarily will as well.

It's definitely possible that the status of indices may change as Curator tasks run. But it seems like Curator may benefit from the option of caching the index metadata between tasks, perhaps as a seperate "task" that generates all of the index metadata initially so it can be reused in subsequent tasks (as part of a "use_cached_metadata" option for example)

issue_title: API objects can't make round trip
issue_body:
This may not be true for all objects retrieved via the various `get()` methods, but take attaching a data disk to a `VirtualMachine` instance for example. To attach a data disk to a VM, the process seems like it should be:

1. get the VM from the API
` vm = compute_client.virtual_machines.get(resource_group_name, vm_name)`

2. modify the local instance by appending a data disk to the VM
`vm.storage_profile.data_disks.append(data_disk)`

3. update the VM
`compute_client.virtual_machines.create_or_update(resource_group_name, vm)`

However, the following error is generated from API when attempting the above:

```
'{"error":{"code":"InvalidRequestContent","message":"The request content was invalid and could not be deserialized: \\'Could not find member \\'resources\\' on object of type \\'ResourceDefinition\\'. Path \\'resources\\', line 1, position 1507.\\'."}}'
```

This appears to be because the `extensions` property of the VM instance retrieved from the API generates an empty list, rather than a `None` value. When calling `create_or_update()`, the empty list starts to cause problems here:

https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/blob/master/azure-mgmt-compute/azure/mgmt/compute/computemanagement.py#L6697

Ultimately this is where the `resources` object is set (and is what the API is complaining about when the request is submitted):

https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/blob/master/azure-mgmt-compute/azure/mgmt/compute/computemanagement.py#L6805

So it seems like either a) the Python SDK  should treat empty lists (and maybe dictionaries?) the same as a `None` property value or b) the ARM API should handle this case more gracefully.

Obviously the workaround for this particular example is `vm.extensions = None` between the calls to `get()` and `create_or_update()`, but that seems like it shouldn't be necessary.

issue_title: Uploader fails if the full path to the target file doesn't exist
issue_body: Creation of a file in ADLS does not require the underlying path to exist. The create call itself will create the entire path or throw if it can't. The recommendation is to remove this requirement and fail only if the target file itself can't be created (which should still be tested for at the very beginning).

issue_title: Deleting void * is undefined behavior.
issue_body:
In `examples/MultiThreading/b3Win32ThreadSupport.cpp` method `void b3Win32ThreadSupport::stopThreads()` an attempt to delete a void * is made with `delete threadStatus.m_lsMemory;`

I see two approaches to fixing this. First approach, introduce another function to delete the memory indirectly. The second one, add an extra argument to `b3Win32lsMemorySetupFunc`, such that if the argument is null, the memory is allocated and returned otherwise the given memory pointer is released. I'm not very fond of the second one but that's one way of dealing with it.
